title: USD/SGD: Upside risks as Hormuz crisis persists – OCBC
body: OCBC strategists Sim Moh Siong and Christopher Wong flag slight upside risks for USD/SGD as the Hormuz standoff weighs on risk appetite and imported cost pressures.

title: PHP: BSP starts new hiking cycle – Commerzbank
body: Commerzbank highlights that BSP raised its policy rate by 25bp to 4.50%, signalling the start of a new tightening cycle to anchor inflation expectations.

title: USD/TWD: Rebound seen as fadeable – OCBC
body: OCBC strategists Sim Moh Siong and Christopher Wong describe a technical rebound in USD/TWD driven by broader US Dollar (USD) strength and risk aversion linked to the US‑Iran ceasefire stalemate.

title: PBoC: Steepening bias as industrial activity holds – DBS
body: DBS Group Research economist Samuel Tse analyses recent steepening in Chinese Yuan (CNY) rates, linking it to a ceasefire between the United States (US) and Iran and stronger-than-expected Q1 growth in China.

title: USD/CHF Price Forecast: Rejected at 100-day SMA, eyes on 0.7800
body: USD/CHF dropped on Friday but finished the week with gains of over 0.35%, trading at 0.7841 as market participants grew confident that US-Iran talks could resume over the weekend to resolve the conflict.

title: USD/IDR: Upside risks widen on fiscal concerns – MUFG
body: Lloyd Chan at MUFG highlights that USD/IDR has broken to fresh highs, overshooting earlier expectations for near-term stabilization. The move is attributed more to domestic confidence and fiscal uncertainty than broad US Dollar (USD) strength.

title: Silver Price Analysis: XAG advances but stalls at technical confluence near $75
body: Silver (XAG/USD) price edges up over 0.50% during Friday’s session, after bouncing off a daily low of $73.95. Speculation about a resumption of negotiations between Washington and Tehran is cheered by investors, which pushed US equities higher in tandem with the precious metals segment.

title: PHP: BSP tightening supports Peso but risks linger – OCBC
body: OCBC strategists Sim Moh Siong and Christopher Wong note the central bank of Philippines Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas' (BSP) 25bp hike to 4.5% and guidance that further increases are possible as inflation forecasts are revised higher and second-round effects emerge.

title: BoC: Cautious hold and two‑sided risks – TD Securities
body: TD Securities strategists, including Andrew Kelvin and colleagues, expect the Bank of Canada to keep the Overnight Rate at 2.25% through the April meeting and likely for the rest of 2026.

title: BoE: MPC holds vigilant stance – Rabobank
body: Rabobank’s Senior Macro Strategist Stefan Koopman expects the Bank of England’s (BoE) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) to leave Bank Rate at 3.75% at the April meeting, maintaining its vigilant stance.

title: Forecasting the upcoming week: US Dollar eases into weekend as markets eye central bank meetings
body: The US Dollar Index (DXY) is losing momentum near the 98.50 price zone and continues drifting lower from recent highs as markets unwind part of the recent US Dollar (USD) rally despite still strong United States (US) data and ongoing Middle East tensions.

title: Gold rebounds as Iran-US deal hopes drive upside
body: Gold (XAU/USD) price recovers some ground on Friday, extending its gains above $4,700 as market sentiment remains upbeat amid headlines from Iran and the US pointing to a resumption of second-round talks aimed at ending the conflict. At the time of writing XAU/USD trades at $4,726, up 0.47%.

title: EUR/USD Weekly Forecast: War and central banks in the limelight
body: The EUR/USD pair ended a three-week winning streak and settled below the 1.1700 mark as the US Dollar (USD) strengthened amid Middle East uncertainty.

title: US Dollar Weekly: Will Kevin Warsh please President Trump?
body: United States President Donald Trump’s nominee to succeed Jerome Powell as Federal Reserve Chair, Kevin Warsh, faced senators, and there was no subtlety.

title: GBP/USD Price Forecast: Bulls seem hesitant as US-Iran tensions counter Fed-BoE divergence
body: The GBP/USD pair edges higher during the first half of the European session on Friday and recovers a part of the previous day's losses to levels just below mid-1.3400s, or a nearly two-week trough.

title: Gold Price Forecast: XAU/USD eyes weekly loss as bears tighten grip
body: Gold is testing the $4,700 level early Friday, holding at its weakest level in over a week and eyeing the first weekly drop in five weeks.

title: Silver Price Forecast: XAG/USD could retest recent lows at $61
body: Silver came under selling pressure in the second half of Thursday, as risk aversion fueled US Dollar (USD) demand.

title: Gold is supposed to be a hedge against war and inflation – Not this time
body: Current global circumstances should be highly favorable for Gold, which functions as a safe-haven asset when geopolitical tensions arise and also acts as a store of value when fiat currencies decline during prolonged inflationary periods. That’s a nice theory, but this isn’t happening now.

title: USD/JPY Price Forecast: Approaches 160.00 intervention threshold amid US-Iran tensions
body: The USD/JPY pair builds on last week's bounce from the vicinity of mid-157.00s and prolongs its uptrend for the fourth straight day on Thursday.

title: Gold Price Forecast: XAU/USD awaits a clear impetus amid US-Iran stand-off
body: Gold is challenging the $4,700 level on renewed downside in Asia on Thursday, wiping out the previous rebound.

title: Ten killed in Ukraine after major Russia drone attack, as RAF deploys Typhoon jets
body: Russia has launched a major drone attack overnight on Ukraine, killing 10 people and injuring more ‌than 30 others, as RAF Typhoon fighter jets were deployed over Romania.

title: Terrorists launch simultaneous attacks in Mali, authorities say
body: Terrorists have carried out attacks in various locations across Mali, according to the country's army.

title: Princess of Wales lays wreath at Cenotaph to mark Anzac Day
body: The Princess of Wales has laid a wreath at the Cenotaph to mark Anzac Day, which commemorates all Australians and New Zealanders who died in conflict.

title: Putin's approval rating falls to lowest since Ukraine invasion, as Kremlin says he may attend G20 after Trump invite
body: President Vladimir Putin's approval rating has fallen to its lowest level since the invasion of Ukraine in February 2022, state polling figures show.

title: Pentagon's Falklands email makes Charles's US visit even more challenging
body: Mention the Falklands War and dig up the issue of America's support over them and something deep inside the British psyche goes off like a landmine.
